old_claim.note: protein_engineering was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for pam_specificity_engineering without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: Altered PAM-specificity variants are engineered Cas9 proteins produced through structural design, selection, and directed evolution.

source_shape.source_locator: Methods and variant-design discussion of engineered Cas9 proteins produced through protein mutation, selection, and characterization.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper describes altered PAM specificity as a protein-engineering result: Cas9 variants are changed and selected to recognize different PAM sequences.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is required because the technology is an engineered protein variant family, not simply a CRISPR application that benefits from protein knowledge.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: pam_specificity_engineering -> protein_engineering remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The source did not describe engineered Cas9 protein variants.
The edge was kept required after rescoping the node away from protein variant engineering.
The receipt claimed only historical influence instead of component-level protein modification.
short_reason: Engineered PAM specificity is implemented through altered Cas9 protein variants.
